items.title: 3 Dow Jones Stocks Billionaires Were Piling Into This Year
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: Billionaire investors are buying stocks in Amazon, Sherwin-Williams, and Nike, betting on their long-term growth potential. Amazon's cloud business and e-commerce dominance, Sherwin-Williams' competitive advantages, and Nike's turnaround plans are attracting these savvy investors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Amazon's industry-leading cloud services business and e-commerce dominance are driving strong financial results, making it an attractive long-term investment for billionaire investors like Chase Coleman.

Sherwin-Williams has an excellent track record of delivering returns to shareholders and is well-positioned to benefit from a rebound in home remodeling and lower interest rates, attracting investments from billionaire managers like Ole Andreas Halvorsen and Chase Coleman.

Despite a challenging year, Nike's plans to refocus on sports and the hiring of a new CEO with fresh ideas are seen as positive catalysts for the company's turnaround, making it an attractive investment for billionaire investor Bill Ackman.
